name: The 'Mantegna Tarocchi' — A Ladder of Being (NOT a Tarot), c. 1465
description:
# Mantegna "Tarocchi" — *not* a tarot

## At a glance

A set of fifty 15th-century engravings, **not a card game and not a tarot deck at all**.
Included in this library precisely to explain the confusion: the name is a later error.
Not by Mantegna, not tarot, not for play or divination — a **didactic image cycle** of
the cosmos and the social order.

## Origin & dating

Two sets survive, the **E-series** (c.1465, the older) and the **S-series** (a little
later). Documentary support includes a Bolognese manuscript dated 1467. Stylistic links
to the Salone dei Mesi at the Palazzo Schifanoia point to artists in the **Ferrarese /
Este** orbit (around Francesco del Cossa); the true engraver is unknown. The attribution
to Andrea Mantegna is spurious.

## Provenance & evidence

Fifty engravings in **five decades** of ten: social ranks (Beggar → Pope), the Muses and
Apollo, the liberal arts, the virtues and cosmic principles, and the celestial spheres.
It is a **ladder of being** — a Renaissance encyclopedia in pictures.

## Game or divination?

**Neither.** No suits, no trick-taking structure, no fortune-telling. Best understood as
**educational prints**, plausibly for instructing upper-class youths — though, honestly,
no document records their actual use. The "Tarocchi" label was pinned on later by writers
who confused them with the picture-rich tarot game.

## The fear question

**Not applicable** — these are humanist study images, openly produced and circulated.
Their inclusion here is corrective: to stop the genealogy absorbing material that merely
*looks* tarot-adjacent.

## Counter-voices

Esoteric authors have repeatedly raided the Mantegna series for "hidden" Neoplatonic or
Hermetic keys to tarot. The series *is* genuinely Neoplatonic — that was mainstream
learned culture — but it is **not an ancestor of the tarot pack**. Treating it as one is
how false genealogies (and the Egyptian story) get built.

## Open questions / corrections owed

- Function (teaching aid? collector's cycle? devotional ladder?) is inferred, not
  documented.
- Relationship, if any, between its iconographic program and early trionfi trumps is a
  matter of influence, not descent.

## Sources & further reading

- "Mantegna Tarocchi," Wikipedia; "Tarocchi di Mantegna, c.1465," World of Playing Cards.
- "The Mantegna Tarocchi and the View of the World in Northern Italy," academia.edu.

---

illustrations.artist: Master of the E-Series Tarocchi (North Italian)
illustrations.edition: Mantegna Tarocchi, E-series, c. 1465 (Cleveland Museum of Art impression)

sections.Depicts: A ragged beggar with two dogs — the lowest condition of man.
sections.On the Ladder: Number 1 of 50, in the series of E · The Estates of Man (1–10). The Mantegna series is a single ascent from the Beggar (1) to the First Cause (50).
sections.Note: not a tarot: These 50 engravings are NOT a tarot — no suits, no trumps, no game. They are a Neoplatonic teaching ladder, mislabelled 'Tarocchi' by later collectors.

sections.Note: not a tarot: These 50 engravings are NOT a tarot — no suits, no trumps, no game. They are a Neoplatonic teaching ladder, mislabelled 'Tarocchi' by later collectors.

sections.About this series: The ten conditions of humankind, from the Beggar to the Pope — the social ladder that begins the ascent.
Apollo and the nine Muses — the inspired arts that lift the soul above mere estate.
Grammar to Theology — the trivium, quadrivium, and the higher sciences, the disciplines of the mind.
The three world-daemons — the living spirits of the Sun, of Time, and of the World.
The four cardinal and three theological virtues — the moral perfection of the soul.
The seven planetary spheres rising to the fixed stars, the Prime Mover, and the First Cause — the cosmos itself, ending in God.

sections.The whole ascent: Read in order, the 50 plates form one continuous Neoplatonic ascent: from the Beggar at the bottom of human society, up through the estates, the inspiring Muses, the disciplines of knowledge, the cosmic genii and the seven virtues, then the planetary spheres, to the fixed stars, the Prime Mover, and finally the First Cause — God. It is a map of the soul's climb from matter to the divine.
sections.Why it is here (and why it is NOT a taro: The 'Mantegna Tarocchi' is the most famous false relative of the tarot: a 15th-century Italian engraving set, often shelved beside tarot and even called 'Tarocchi', but with no suits, no 22 trumps, and no game. (It is also not by Mantegna.) Its imagery — virtues, planets, estates — overlaps the tarot trumps because both drew on the same Renaissance iconographic vocabulary, which is exactly why the two are confused. Including it here lets the genealogy say clearly: this is a cousin in imagery, not an ancestor in fact.
